mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and retrieval of unstructured data, such as AI and machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes for deployment and the complexity of managing distributed systems.

steps.title: Add the Milvus Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Milvus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

```

> This is the official Helm repository for Milvus.

steps.title: Install Milvus CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Milvus to function properly in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

```

steps.title: Install Milvus using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Milvus in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

steps.title: Create a Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an example Milvus cluster resource to manage the Milvus deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: "2.5.0"
EOF

```
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: "2.5.0"
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Milvus cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get milvusclusters -n milvus

```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Milvus to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.6.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to be drained.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Milvus service is not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods with 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Milvus cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Milvus cluster is still initializing or has failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Milvus pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources allocated for Milvus.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements of 4 vCPU and 8GB RAM.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions for Milvus have not been installed.

**Fix:**
Re-run the CRD installation command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old Milvus API v1.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.4, ensure to migrate your data using the provided migration tools.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus cluster status as 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get milvusclusters -n milvus'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: "2.5.0"
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
